note: Run registry for the CPU lexical baselines (these do not go through run_manifest.py). Failed runs are retained, never overwritten, and their output directories are not reused.

runs.purpose: First full-target-set lexical baseline (name_match + bm25 x 3 folds x 3 views).
runs.log: logs/lexical_baselines_full.log (subsequently truncated by the replacement run; the exit line LEXFULL_EXIT=143 and partial results were read before truncation)

termination.cause: Operator-issued `pkill -f run_lexical_baseline` while relaunching to pick up the exact/tolerant CSR hash change. The kill and the relaunch were issued in one compound command, so the replacement raced the kill and never started; the job was left dead with the log frozen at 07:38 UTC.
termination.pid: not captured (the launcher did not record the PID; only the process name was matched). Fixed for later runs by logging the PID at launch.

runs.progress_at_termination: completed through fold1_seed42/bm25/missing; fold2_seed79 not run

outputs.reason: the script serializes results only after all combinations finish

runs.provenance_gap: started before the sparse_matrix_exact_hash / sparse_matrix_tolerant_hash change, so its partial numbers lack publication-grade CSR provenance and are not citable.

runs.purpose: Clean restart: first citable lexical result (ledger R-042).
runs.command: bash scripts/baselines/launch_run.sh LEX-RUN-002 $HOME/research_kg/DBP5L/ind_v2/audits/lexical python3 scripts/baselines/run_lexical_baseline.py --max-targets 0 --out-dir DBP5L/ind_v2/audits/lexical/LEX-RUN-002
includes: full target set per fold (no sampling)
exact + tolerant CSR hashes with recorded rounding precision
vocabulary/tokenization, candidate-order, view and targets hashes
runs.output_directory: DBP5L/ind_v2/audits/lexical/LEX-RUN-002/ (fresh; LEX-RUN-001's path is not reused)

runs.purpose: B0 anchor baseline, DBP5L-Ind v2 fold0_seed13, training seed 42.

termination.cause: Bug in launch_run.sh: $LOG was referenced inside the detached shell but never exported (only RUN_ID/RUN_DIR/HEARTBEAT_SECONDS were), so `"$@" >> "$LOG"` redirected to an empty filename and the shell exited 1 before the job started. No run.log was produced. The lock+heartbeat launcher rewrite had never been exercised (only check_run.sh was tested).

runs.detected_by: check_run.sh reported FINISHED exit_code=1 at the pre-unattended health check
runs.fix: export LOG into the detached shell + `set -u` so an unset variable fails loudly; added scripts/baselines/test_launch_run.sh (10 checks incl. log capture, exit/signal recording, lock, heartbeat, reuse refusal, checker agreement).

runs.purpose: B0 anchor baseline, DBP5L-Ind v2 fold0_seed13, seed 42.

termination.primary: torch.AcceleratorError: CUDA error: unknown error (cudaErrorUnknown) on the first hard-negative matmul at epoch-5 HN onset (HN cache built fine, 1.2min/174MB immediately before). ENVIRONMENTAL, not code: the same code ran 5 epochs + the cache build successfully, and a post-mortem CUDA matmul on the same GPU succeeds (15.5GB free). Most likely a WSL2 CUDA-context disturbance during the session login/reconnect churn around 11:00Z.
termination.secondary: bash then emitted syntax errors on run_b0_fold0_seed42.sh lines 21-22 because the script had been EDITED (rewritten to the explicit-resume version) WHILE bash was executing it -> bash resumed at a stale byte offset. Cosmetic; did NOT cause the CUDA abort (python is an independent child).
termination.CORRECTION: Earlier 'environmental cudaErrorUnknown' diagnosis was WRONG. B0-RUN-003 died identically at epoch 5 with a clear 'CUDA out of memory' in build_entity_cache; WSL had masked the same OOM as 'unknown error' here. Root cause: build_entity_cache called model.encode WITHOUT torch.no_grad(), accumulating a 568M-param autograd graph across all 83 batches. Deterministic, not transient.

runs.process_lesson: Do not edit files a running job executes. Training code will be frozen (git tag) for the duration of the B0 matrix; the run script is copied immutably at launch.
runs.recovery: Fresh B0-RUN-003 from current HEAD (single immutable commit with all fixes: selection-epoch persistence, complete cache key, HN provenance). ~2.3h lost.

runs.purpose: B0 anchor baseline retry from tagged code.
runs.progress: 5/30 epochs; died at epoch-5 HN cache build

termination.cause: CUDA out of memory in build_entity_cache (train_dbp5l_lora.py:185). ROOT CAUSE: model.encode ran without torch.no_grad() -> a 568M-param autograd graph accumulated across all 83 encode batches (emb.cpu() keeps the GPU graph alive), OOMing on top of the ~9GB training baseline. Reciprocal training raised the baseline enough to expose a latent bug the older non-reciprocal Run E tolerated.
termination.reproduced: test_hn_oom.py OOMs on the grad-ON path with only 4,000 entities in a fresh process; fixed no_grad path builds the full 42,450 universe at 3.01GB peak.

runs.fix: wrap the encode loop in torch.no_grad() + torch.cuda.empty_cache() before build. Verified: test_hn_fix.py builds 42,450 entities at 3.01GB.
runs.replaced_by: B0-RUN-004 (from fixed code, new tag b0-baseline-v2)

runs.purpose: B0 anchor baseline, fixed HN code.
runs.progress: 26/30 epochs completed (best_epoch 23, best_acc1 27.42); last_checkpoint.pt at epoch 26

termination.cause: EXTERNAL: another GPU LLM was launched by the operator and its memory collided with training -> CUDA OOM at epoch 26. NOT our code (the HN no_grad fix held for 21 clean refreshes, epochs 5-26). GPU free again post-mortem.

runs.code_delta: training code (train_dbp5l_lora.py/eval_dbp5l.py/run_manifest.py/run script) BYTE-IDENTICAL to launch: git diff b0-baseline-v2..HEAD = empty; only docs/test commits since. Epochs 27-30 scientifically continuous.
runs.note: Continues in the SAME checkpoint dir as B0-RUN-004; resume restored weights/optimizer/scheduler/RNG/best_epoch. ~4 epochs (~2h) remaining.

termination.cause: External OOM AGAIN 2min after resume — the other GPU LLM was still running. Died during epoch 26 (before any epoch completed), so last_checkpoint.pt unchanged (still epoch 26, sha 5997fcf4ea116e90...). No progress lost or corrupted.

runs.note: Second resume of the same intact epoch-26 checkpoint (sha unchanged across both prior external OOMs). ~4 epochs remaining. GPU must stay clear of other jobs.
runs.result: PASS — all 12 concept-clustered tests significant vs R-042 lexical (min lo95 +0.79)

runs.note: Fold path only differs from fold0 script; ETA ~13h.

termination.cause: Operator stopped it manually (SIGILL) at epoch 6. Not a code fault.

runs.note: Resumed after operator manual stop. Train code == b0-baseline-v2. ~11h left.

termination.cause: Operator stopped manually (SIGILL) at epoch 12. Not code.

cudaErrorUnknown at train loop (line 672) epoch 27 — NOT the HN path (cache built fine at ep27). Transient WSL CUDA-context hiccup class (same as RUN-002 original). GPU passes post-mortem matmul (15.7GB free); 2GB held-with-no-process was a post-abort artifact, cleared.

cudaErrorUnknown at train loop epoch 28 — third such crash near ep27-28. Diagnosis: a Windows-side GPU process (python PID 532 ~12GB and/or ChatGPT.exe) holds ~2GB persistently (survived wsl --shutdown) and likely spikes, colliding with training. NOT our code/GPU-hardware (42C, no throttle, CUDA sanity passes).

runs.note: 3 epochs left; WSL restarted, GPU cleared to 15GB free

termination.cause: 4th cudaErrorUnknown ep28 — OpenAI Codex/ChatGPT.exe GPU collision (confirmed: closing it let RUN-012 pass ep28)

runs.note: ChatGPT desktop closed -> passed the ep28 crash point (step 200+). 3 epochs left.

milestones.milestone: epoch-5 gate CLEARED on the real run
milestones.evidence: HN cache built at epoch 5 (1.2min, shape (42450,1024), adapter a7461f7459e1) AND optimization resumed — now epoch 6/30 training normally. The exact point B0-RUN-002 and B0-RUN-003 OOMed.
milestones.status_note: R-F003/R-F004 remain OPEN. The HN no_grad bug is fixed+verified, but the runs are marked resolved only when B0-RUN-004 completes all 30 epochs + writes its selection_record.json. Verification GPU overlap affected timing (~30min), not validity.

incidents.note: Run statuses are NOT modified by this resolution — failed runs stay failed (append-only).
